framework.name: DO-178C — Airborne Software Safety (DAL A–E)
framework.description: Aviation software safety standard for flight computers, avionics, engine controllers, autopilot, and fly-by-wire systems. Covers Design Assurance Levels (DAL) from A (catastrophic) to E (no effect), with structural coverage, traceability, and verification requirements per RTCA DO-178C and EUROCAE ED-12C.

rules.title: No goto usage in safety-critical code
rules.description: DO-178C Table A-7 prohibits goto for DAL A/B software as it creates unstructured control flow that defeats static analysis and makes certification evidence generation infeasible.
rules.fix: Replace goto with structured control flow (while/for loops, if/else, or state machines).

rules.title: No recursion in avionics software
rules.description: Recursion makes worst-case stack depth analysis impossible, violating DO-178C stack usage requirements for DAL A/B/C.
rules.fix: Convert recursive algorithms to iterative equivalents with explicit stack management and bounded depth.

rules.title: No dynamic memory allocation
rules.description: malloc/free violate DO-178C determinism requirements and introduce fragmentation/exhaustion risks unacceptable in flight-critical software.
rules.fix: Use static allocation, stack allocation, or preallocated memory pools with compile-time bounds.

rules.title: Shared state must be volatile
rules.description: Variables shared between ISRs and main code must be volatile to prevent compiler optimizations that violate DO-178C concurrency requirements.
rules.fix: Mark all interrupt-shared variables as volatile and document the concurrency protocol.

rules.title: ISR must not call blocking functions
rules.description: Interrupt handlers calling printf, malloc, mutex_lock, or I/O violate DO-178C timing determinism for DAL A/B/C systems.
rules.fix: Move blocking operations to deferred work queues or task-level handlers.

rules.title: Functions must propagate error returns
rules.description: Ignoring non-void function returns violates DO-178C error handling requirements — every failure path must be explicitly handled.
rules.fix: Check all function returns and propagate errors to calling layers or handle with recovery logic.

rules.title: Polling loops must have timeout
rules.description: Unbounded polling can cause infinite hangs in flight control, violating DO-178C availability requirements for DAL A/B systems.
rules.fix: Add timeout counter and recovery handling: if(timeout==0) { /* enter safe mode */ }

rules.title: Timeout must have recovery handler
rules.description: Timeouts without recovery checks allow flight systems to continue operating in a failed state, violating DO-178C fault containment.
rules.fix: Add explicit recovery after timeout loop: if(timeout==0) { /* NVIC_SystemReset(), enter safe state, or failover */ }

rules.title: Missing watchdog refresh
rules.description: Flight control loops without watchdog refresh risk undetected hangs, violating DO-178C monitoring requirements for DAL A systems.
rules.fix: Add IWDG_Refresh() or WDT_Feed() in every control loop iteration.

rules.title: ARINC 429 word without SSM/SDI validation
rules.description: ARINC 429 data words must validate Sign/Status Matrix (SSM) and Source/Destination Identifier (SDI) to prevent use of invalid avionics data.
rules.fix: Check SSM bits (29-30): if((word>>29 & 0x3) == 0) { /* Failure Warning */ } and validate SDI (bits 9-10).

rules.title: Flight control law without saturation limiter
rules.description: Control surface commands (pitch/roll/yaw) without saturation can exceed physical limits, causing structural damage or loss of control.
rules.fix: Add saturation: cmd = CLAMP(cmd, MIN_DEF, MAX_DEF); or use hardware limiters with software pre-check.

rules.title: Air data computation without cross-check
rules.description: Airspeed, altitude, and Mach number from a single ADC without cross-validation violates DO-178C redundancy for DAL A systems.
rules.fix: Implement 2-out-of-3 voting, reasonableness checks, or RAIM (Receiver Autonomous Integrity Monitoring).

rules.title: Engine FADEC parameter without range limiting
rules.description: Full Authority Digital Engine Control (FADEC) commands without range checks can exceed turbine limits, risking engine failure.
rules.fix: Add range checks: if(N1_cmd > N1_MAX || N1_cmd < N1_MIN) { /* reject command */ }

rules.title: MIL-STD-1553 message without RT address validation
rules.description: MIL-STD-1553 bus commands without Remote Terminal (RT) address validation can route to wrong avionics LRUs.
rules.fix: Validate RT address: if(rt_addr < 1 || rt_addr > 31) { /* reject */ }

rules.title: ARINC 653 partition without health monitor
rules.description: ARINC 653 partitioned RTOS tasks without health monitoring violate DO-178C fault containment for DAL A Integrated Modular Avionics (IMA).
rules.fix: Register health monitor callback: ARINC653_RegisterHMCallback() and handle ERROR_HANDLER states.

rules.title: Navigation data without integrity check
rules.description: GPS/IRS navigation solutions without RAIM or FDE (Fault Detection and Exclusion) violate DO-178C integrity requirements for DAL A systems.
rules.fix: Implement RAIM (Receiver Autonomous Integrity Monitoring), FDE, or multi-sensor fusion with consistency checks.

rules.title: Fly-by-wire actuator without position feedback
rules.description: Flight control actuators without LVDT position feedback violate DO-178C closed-loop requirements for DAL A fly-by-wire systems.
rules.fix: Implement closed-loop feedback: error = cmd - LVDT_Read(); if(abs(error) > TOL) { /* fault */ }

rules.title: Landing gear sequence without WOW check
rules.description: Landing gear retraction without Weight-On-Wheels (WOW) switch check can retract gear on ground, causing aircraft collapse.
rules.fix: Check WOW before retraction: if(!WOW_OnGround && gear_handle == RETRACT) { /* allow */ }
